questions.description: "The old rule in Pervical v Wright [1902] 2 Ch 421 that directors' duties are owed to the company and not to its…
questions.text: "The old rule in Pervical v Wright [1902] 2 Ch 421 that directors' duties are owed to the company and not to its shareholders, creditors or employees is becoming less and less relevant." You are required to discuss this statement citing all relevant case and statute law in your answer.

questions.description: "The Court's jurisdiction to wind up a company under Section 213(e) on the grounds that it is unable to pay its debts…
questions.text: "The Court's jurisdiction to wind up a company under Section 213(e) on the grounds that it is unable to pay its debts is discretionary and only exercised in the case of a bona fide debt." You are required to discuss this statement citing all relevant case and statute law in your answer.

questions.description: William's World of Colours Limited (the "Company") is a company engaged in… Company, Director.
questions.text: William's World of Colours Limited (the "Company") is a company engaged in the business of manufacturing and distributing paint to the Irish construction industry. For the past number of years business has been booming. Two of the Company's customers have had lucrative contracts for the painting of new housing estates. The Company has three directors -- William, Peggy and Eugene -- and is a single-member private limited company, all shares being held by William. William is the son of the Company's founder and is the managing director. Peggy, his daughter, is the Sales Director and Eugene is her husband. An accountant by profession, Eugene is effectively a non-executive director who works in a local bank. His involvement in the company is very limited. The slow-down in the building sector in 2007 badly affected the Company. William realised things were bad when the Company's bank manager telephoned him in September to say that the Company had exceeded its overdraft. William persuaded the bank to extend the overdraft to pay wages and give him time to collect some outstanding debts. The Company chugged along until December when William realised that the Company was insolvent and, without any hope of an immediate improvement being in sight, William placed the company into creditors' voluntary liquidation. The Company's liquidator found that the Company's record keeping was sporadic and that management accounts had not been prepared with the frequency that would have alerted the directors early on that there was a problem which, the liquidator discovered, could have been ascertained as far back as June 2007. You are required to advise on the likelihood that each of the three directors will be made the subject of a restriction order pursuant to Section 150 of the Companies Act 1990. In your answer you must cite all relevant case and statute law.

questions.description: Dan was an employee and minority shareholder in XYZ Construction Limited… Company, Shareholder.
questions.text: Dan was an employee and minority shareholder in XYZ Construction Limited (the "Company") which operated as a construction company in Howth. Following the tragic death of Dan in a road traffic accident his widow, Diana, became entitled under Dan's will to his estate, including his shares in the Company. As an employee of the Company and in recognition of his contribution to the Company's success, over a number of years, Dan had been given shares in the Company and at his death he held 500 shares, amounting to 5% of the Company's issued share capital. The Company's Directors, PJ and Des, became concerned when Diana's solicitor notified the Company that Diana intends to become registered as a shareholder. The issue for them is that Diana's family operate a rival construction company in Howth, and PJ and Des are not at all keen that she would become entitled to receive financial information about the Company. (Dan had always assured them that he never disclosed any information to his wife about the Company's affairs). The Company's articles of association empower the directors, in their absolute discretion, to refuse to register any transfer or transmission of shares without assigning any reason. You are required to advise Diana on whether she has a right to become registered as a shareholder in the Company and on her remedies if she is denied registration. In your answer you must cite all relevant case and statute law.

questions.description: "Part III of the Companies Act 1990 concerning transactions between companies and their directors over-regulates the…
questions.text: "Part III of the Companies Act 1990 concerning transactions between companies and their directors over-regulates the relations between directors and their companies and operates to prevent many bona fide commercial transactions". You are required to discuss this statement citing all relevant case and statute law in your answer.

questions.description: Gordon is the liquidator of Fabulous Foods Limited (the "Company") which… Company, Director.
questions.text: Gordon is the liquidator of Fabulous Foods Limited (the "Company") which has been placed into official liquidation following the bringing of a petition by the revenue commissioners grounded on the Company's inability to pay its debts. Gordon seeks your advice on the validity of the following transactions: a. Eighteen months before the presentation of the petition, the Company had created a floating charge in favour of its sole member and managing director, John, to secure a loan of €50,000 made by John to the Company. There is evidence that the loan made by John was advanced in cash the day after the floating charge was created. b. Two cheques were drawn and dated one month before the petition was presented to the High Court to have the Company would up. The first cheque for €15,000 was payable to John and represented the repayment of part of the loan made 18 months earlier. This cheque was cashed the same day it was drawn. The second cheque for €30,000, which was payable to a creditor of the company for goods purchased by the Company, was cashed the day after the petition was presented. You are required to advise Gordon on the transaction described in (a) and (b) and in your answer you must cite all relevant case and statute law.

questions.description: A fixed charge attaches specifically to the charged property from the…
questions.text: A fixed charge attaches specifically to the charged property from the moment it is created or acquired by the chargor whereas, by contrast, a floating charge does not attach to specific property prior to crystallisation". You are required to discuss this statement citing all relevant case and statute law in your answer.

questions.description: Roderick and Robert operate a roofing business and employ eight people. Company.
questions.text: Roderick and Robert operate a roofing business and employ eight people. Their work involves putting tiles and slates on the roofs of houses and industrial premises. By its nature the work is dangerous and despite being committed to safe work practices, there have been several accidents over the years. Most recently one of their employees fell from a roof in Ranelagh and broke his back, leaving him paraplegic. Roderick's and Robert's insurance covered the €600,000 compensation which was required to be paid to the injured employee. However, in addition to a big increase in their insurance premium, Roderick and Robert were told by the insurance company that their cover was being capped at €500,000 for this year. The business is currently organised as a partnership and Roderick and Robert fear that their personal assets may be exposed were there to be another serious accident. You are required to advise Roderick and Robert on the advantages [and]{.underline} consequences of incorporating their business as a private company limited by shares. In your answer you are required to cite all relevant case and statute law.

questions.description: "Corporate capacity may be classified as comprised of substantive objects, express ancillary powers or implied…
questions.text: "Corporate capacity may be classified as comprised of substantive objects, express ancillary powers or implied ancillary powers". You are required to discuss this statement citing all relevant case and statute law in your answer.

questions.description: "While shares in companies are prima facie transferable, a private company is required by law to restrict the right to…
questions.text: "While shares in companies are prima facie transferable, a private company is required by law to restrict the right to transfer its shares, which restriction will usually take the form of either a right of pre-emption on transfer or a discretion vested in the directors to refuse the registration of share transfers". You are required to discuss this statement citing all relevant case and statute law in your answer.

questions.description: Fred is a director of Rental Property Management Limited (the "Company")… Fiduciary, Company.
questions.text: Fred is a director of Rental Property Management Limited (the "Company") which has operated a business in Cork which assists landlords rent their investment properties. Fred is not a shareholder in the Company which is owned by the Jones family although Fred has worked in the Company for 8 years. At first Fred worked in an administrative capacity but subsequently, after he proved himself to be very proficient in getting more landlords on to the Company's books, he was appointed a company director with responsibility for sales. James Jones, the Company's Managing Director and head of the Jones family, which owns the shares in the Company approaches you for advice. It has come to his knowledge that Fred has been "double-jobbing" for the last two years and has set up an internet site which provides very similar services to landlords to those provided by the Company, the website being owned by a company that is fronted by Fred's wife, Freda. James Jones is concerned that business opportunities which would have gone the way of the Company have instead been diverted to Fred and Freda's company. He is also concerned that confidential information belonging to the Company many have been utilised by Fred. James Jones also tells you that some time ago Fred had urged the Company to establish its own website to service landlords but that the Company put the idea on hold. You are required to advise James Jones on the law relating to directors' common law and equitable fiduciary duties and on the Company's remedies against Fred. In your answer you must cite all relevant case and statute law and attempt to apply these to the facts provided above.

questions.description: You are approached for advice by Denis, the financial director of a medium… Regulation, Company.
questions.text: You are approached for advice by Denis, the financial director of a medium sized company called Festive Tidings Limited (the "Company"), which distributes Christmas greeting cards. The Company has three directors (of which Denis is one) and is wholly-owned subsidiary of an English company called Festive Greetings (UK) Limited which holds the subsidiary Company's only issued share which has a nominal value of €1. Denis tells you that he and his fellow directors are coming under increasing pressure to sanction a dividend distribution to the holding company -- which is in some financial difficulties -- in the sum of €1 million. Denis is concerned about this because while the Company is solvent and would be solvent after the payment of the €1 million to the holding company, the Company has only €500,000 in available distributable profits. Notwithstanding this, Denis is concerned that the holding company may pass a resolution directing the directors of the Company to pay the €1 million sought. The Company's Articles of Association are in the form of Table A and include Regulation 116 on the recommendation and declaration of dividends. You are required to advise Denis and his fellow directors on the law relating to distributions and to the rights, duties and liabilities of directors and shareholders in relation to distributions. In your answer you must cite all relevant case and statute law and attempt to apply these to the facts provided above.

questions.description: "When proceedings are brought against the director of an insolvent company under Section 150 of the Companies Act 1990,…
questions.text: "When proceedings are brought against the director of an insolvent company under Section 150 of the Companies Act 1990, case law indicates that unless the director can show that he or she acted honestly and responsibly in relation to the conduct of the affairs of the company, the Court will make a restriction order". You are required to discuss this statement citing all relevant case and statute law in your answer.

questions.description: Cowboy Farm Caterers Limited (the "Company") had a very successful… Company, Director.
questions.text: Cowboy Farm Caterers Limited (the "Company") had a very successful business supplying food to building sites across Dublin City and County. The economic slowdown in the building sector began to hurt the Company in April, and since then things have gone from bad to worse. Whereas previously, another site opened up somewhere else when a building site closed following the completion of a development, since April there have been far fewer new sites opening. Even where the Company goes on to a site, sales are not being made as there is a growing tendency for the workers to bring their own packed lunches rather than buy the Company's products such as its "Bull-burgers" and "Pig-Patty" all- day breakfast rolls. Increasingly, the Company's directors, Tommy and Gretta, have had to borrow on the Company's overdraft to pay its employees and suppliers. PAYE and PRSI due in respect of the Company's own employees has not been remitted to the revenue commissioners, and a considerable sum is outstanding to the meat producers who supply the Company. Matters have been exacerbated because the Company's books and records are very deficient, the Company never having hired a proper accountant to prepare books of account. Having claimed the audit exemption, the Company dismissed its auditors and cobbled together an incomplete accounting document which was filled in the Companies Registration Office. From April to September Tommy and Gretta continued to trade in the hope that business would pick up. Throughout the months of July, August and September the Company was operating at a loss. The directors continued to incur credit from suppliers although by the end of August there was little or no prospect of the creditors ever being repaid. Following the presentation of a petition by the Revenue Commissioners to have the company placed into financial liquidation, Gordon was appointed official liquidator. You are required to advise Gordon on Tommy's and Gretta's liabilities as directors in relation to reckless and fraudulent trading and the failure to keep proper books of account. In your answer you must cite all relevant case and statute law and attempt to apply these to the facts provided above. (NOTE TO CANDIDATES: In attempting to answer this question you are directed specifically not to address the law relating to the restriction and disqualification of directors).

questions.description: "Retention of title clauses in contracts for the sale of goods to companies can in some, but not in all circumstances,…
questions.text: "Retention of title clauses in contracts for the sale of goods to companies can in some, but not in all circumstances, constitute the charges that, in order to be valid, require to be registered under Section 99 of the Companies Act 1963". You are required to discuss this statement citing all relevant case and statute law in your answer.

questions.description: Dickensian Times Limited (the "Company") is a private limited company… Consideration, Company.
questions.text: Dickensian Times Limited (the "Company") is a private limited company owned by Tim and Estella who are its only directors. The Company has been in the antique business for 10 years and has operated a retail shop in Francis Street. The Company has a term loan and current account with Big Bank plc (the "Bank") both of which have been guaranteed by Tim and Estella. Both loans were also secured by a debenture incorporating a fixed charge over the Company's leasehold interest in the Francis Street shop as well as a floating charge over the Company's stock. Sales were really poor in 2008 and the Company fell into substantial arrears with its loans of some €2 million. The Company also owes Tim and Estella some €100,000 as they loaned this money to the Company upon its incorporation. The Company has no other creditors. Despite all efforts to restructure the loans and save the Company, in April the Bank was forced to appoint a receiver (James) to dispose of the Company's stock and premises. Tim and Estella believed that the leasehold interest was worth €50,000 and that the stock, comprising of antiques, was worth €2,500,000. James, the receiver, sold the premises for €50,000 and announced that he proposed to have a public auction in July to dispose of the antiques. Tim and Estella were horrified to learn this because they believed that he should have waited until November/December to avail of the Christmas market. Despite this James pressed ahead with the auction which realised just €1,500,000 for the Company's stock of antiques. Together with the €50,000 for the lease of the premises, there is shortfall of some €450,000 due by the Company to the Bank. Tim and Estella seek to maintain three claims against the receiver: (a) They allege that the receiver acted in breach of his duties to the Company in disposing of the assets when he did and for the consideration he accepted. (b) Being liable as guarantors of the Company's debts for the outstanding €450,000, Tim and Estella allege that James is in breach of his common law duties to them as guarantors. (c) Tim and Estella also claim that he breached his duties to them as creditors of the Company to the extent that their loan of €100,000 will not now be repaid. You are required to advise James in relation to each of the foregoing claims against him. In your answer you must cite all relevant case and statute law and attempt to apply these to the facts provided above.
